subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: rebuilt from independent sources (UN/World Bank estimates, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, ACLED, UN OCHA/IPC, IOM, OFAC); neither the Houthi nor the recognized-government output used on governance/rights/economics.

De-facto partitioned (a Houthi-controlled north vs the internationally-recognized government/PLC south) with a collapsed, split currency and no unified central bank. Data is fragmentary; confidence is low and figures are external proxies.
Standout drags: an active/frozen civil war, one of the world's worst humanitarian crises, a collapsed split economy, and Houthi Red Sea/Israel attacks drawing strikes. Overall: clearly negative.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May — the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A collapsed, split, aid-dependent economy with a fractured currency.

fiscal_state.notes: Collapsed and split between two rival fiscal authorities, ~80% aid-dependent, the world's largest humanitarian appeal. (World Bank; UN OCHA)

monetary_independence.notes: No unified central bank; the rial collapsed and split into divergent zone exchange rates. (World Bank; ACAPS)

inflation.notes: Very high, severe in the government-held south where the rial collapsed. (World Bank; ACAPS)

banking_stability.notes: Broken and split, with Houthi-area banks sanctioned and SWIFT-cut. (IMF; OFAC)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Oil exports halted since 2022 by drone threats, Red-Sea-blockade dynamics, and import dependence. (World Bank; ACLED)

institutional.notes: No functional unified state, collapsed data, and among the worst press repression.

rule_of_law.notes: No functional unified state - a theocratic-authoritarian north versus a fractured southern coalition. (V-Dem; Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: Collapsed and largely absent - external proxies only. (World Bank caveats)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Collapsed and split, with salaries unpaid for years. (UN OCHA; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Near the RSF bottom, with journalists detained or disappeared by all sides. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: A fragmented civil war and a famine-adjacent humanitarian catastrophe.

political_stability.notes: A fragmented, frozen civil war under a fragile UN truce, with de-facto partition and no settlement. (UN; ICG)

civil_liberties.notes: Severe Houthi repression and escalating gender restrictions, catastrophic in conflict zones. (HRW; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A war state that nonetheless hosts and transits African migrants facing abuse. (IOM; HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: Shattered along north-south, sectarian (Zaydi/Sunni), and tribal lines. (ICG; ACLED)

demographics.notes: Very young and high-fertility, but with a famine/malnutrition catastrophe. (UN; IPC)

geopolitical.notes: A regional-proxy battleground, with Houthi Red Sea/Israel attacks drawing strikes.

alliance_reliability.notes: A proxy battleground - the Houthis Iran-aligned, the government Saudi/UAE-backed. (CFR; ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: Houthi forces attacked Gulf and Red Sea shipping and Saudi targets as part of the 2026 war, on top of the existing civil war. (Al Jazeera; Atlantic Council)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: The Houthis US-designated and sanctioned - isolated with capital controls. (OFAC)

physical_practical.notes: A collapsed health system amid famine, war-destroyed infrastructure, and extreme water scarcity.

climate.notes: Acute water depletion, extreme heat, flood, with readiness collapsed by war/famine - near-existential adaptive failure. (WB; IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Collapsed, with recurrent cholera and famine-adjacent malnutrition - among the world's worst. (WHO; UN OCHA; IPC)

infrastructure.notes: A war-destroyed grid, water, and ports. (World Bank; UN)

crime_safety.notes: War zones, landmines, lawlessness, and kidnapping. (ACLED; UN)

summary: A de-facto partitioned war state - a Houthi-controlled north versus an internationally-recognized but fractured southern coalition - locked in one of the world's worst humanitarian crises. The economy is collapsed and split with no unified central bank and a fractured currency, the health system has collapsed amid recurrent cholera and famine-adjacent malnutrition, and infrastructure is war-destroyed. Houthi Red Sea and Israel attacks have drawn US, UK, and Israeli strikes. Data is fragmentary and figures are external proxies; the picture is clearly negative.
